name: QRadar-0005 - Large-Scale Data Exfiltration Detection
description: Detects potential data exfiltration by monitoring for unusually large outbound data transfers. Triggers when a host transmits more than 500 MB to a destination within a 30-minute window, which may indicate data theft via FTP, HTTP/S, or custom protocols.

detection.aql: SELECT sourceIP, destinationIP, destinationPort, SUM(bytesOut) as total_bytes_out, COUNT(*) as flow_count FROM flows WHERE bytesOut IS NOT NULL AND destinationPort NOT IN (80, 443, 53) GROUP BY sourceIP, destinationIP, destinationPort HAVING SUM(bytesOut) >= 524288000 LAST 30 MINUTES

response.offense_description: A host has transferred more than 500 MB of data within 30 minutes on a non-standard port. May indicate unauthorized data exfiltration.

notes: Inspired by SIGMA rule: net_data_exfiltration_over_non_http. Adjust threshold based on normal business traffic baselines. Removed IsInternal() for compatibility.
author: Security Team
